name: Simple Red Curry Paste
recipeIngredient: 4 Slices of Dried kaffir lime rind (makrut)
2 Stalks of fresh lemon grass, white bottoms only, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. CRACK DRIED CHILES and shake out seeds. Remove and discard stems.
2. Cover pods with warm water for 15 minutes or until soft. Drain well.
3. Soak dried kaffir lime rind in warm water for 15 minutes or until soft.
4. In an ungreased skillet over medium heat, toast coriander and cumin seeds for 3 minutes or until fragrant.
5. When cool, finely grind the coriander and cumin seeds; set aside.
6. Individually grind the reconstituted makrut rind, galangal, lemon grass, and chilies into a fine powder using a spice mill. Remove each mixture and transfer to a mini food processor or blender.
7. Add shallots, garlic, coriander roots, shrimp paste, and reserved coriander to the processor or blender.
8. Process or blend the mixture until smooth.
9. Pour vegetable oil down through the feed tube while blending.
10. Transfer the mixture to a mortar and pound out any lumps into a smooth paste. Makes 1/3 Cup.
